opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My legs are ballooning up, my urine is full of foam, and I can barely walk to the bathroom without losing my breath.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The swelling and breathlessness have got a lot worse over the last week and I'm struggling to get around the house.

symptom_narrative.onset: come on gradually over the past week, worse in the last day or two
symptom_narrative.character: heavy swollen legs and puffiness, foamy urine, and getting puffed out just walking to the toilet
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: bad enough that I can only manage short distances before I have to stop for breath
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: both legs up to the calves, and my breathing
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: there most of the time now and worse when I move about
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: sitting still and resting helps my breathing a bit
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: walking, and it's worse than it was a few days ago
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: feeling wiped out and exhausted, no appetite at all the last couple of days, feeling a bit sick, and up several times a night to pass water
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had a bit of ankle swelling before but never this bad or this breathless
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: only moving short distances because of the breathlessness and the heavy legs; worried about falling

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: diabetes for a long time, and I know my sugars haven't been well controlled
past_medical_history.how_managed: meant to be on insulin but I miss doses
blood pressure tablets, but I've been missing those too

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: blood pressure tablet (one of the ones ending in -pril)

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; struggles to keep on top of the tablets; the pillbox has missed doses; eats a lot of takeaway

access_barriers.other: lives alone with poor medication adherence and a high falls risk from fatigue and heavy legs

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-monitored on an uncalibrated home cuff; patient reports his blood pressure has been running higher than usual

objective_data_offered.value: urine is full of foam / very frothy
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient's own visual description, not a formal urinalysis

objective_data_offered.value: both legs ballooned up and swollen to the calves, skin feels tight
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient/video description; degree and pitting not clinician-graded

objective_data_offered.value: about 94 kg on the bathroom scales, up on my usual
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: home scales; patient reports recent weight gain
